description_long: This house features a symmetrical design with two main rectangular volumes separated by a central open courtyard. The exterior walls are finished in a light-colored brick, and the roof is covered with dark, overlapping shingles in a classic gable style. The front facade is lined with tall, narrow windows and flanked by evenly spaced, columnar evergreen trees, creating a formal and inviting appearance. The rear of the house opens onto a green courtyard, bordered by low walls and additional landscaping. The side and rear elevations have fewer windows, emphasizing privacy and a solid, fortress-like feel. The overall structure suggests a blend of traditional and modern suburban residential architecture.
description: A symmetrical two-story house with a central courtyard, light brick walls, dark gable roofs, and tall front windows framed by evergreen trees.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry, symmetrical windows, and a row of tall evergreen trees flanking the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gable roofs, light brick walls, and minimal windows on the side elevation.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open courtyard, green lawn, and a few windows facing the backyard.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with gable roofs, dark shadowed walls, and limited window openings.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryway with a flat path, flanked by tall evergreen trees and symmetrical windows, no porch or overhang.
